title: eurya quinquelocularis
intro: Eurya quinquelocularis, also known as the five-local Eurya, is a plant species native to tropical Asia, belonging to the family Theaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities. The health benefits associated with Eurya quinquelocularis include the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory infections, as well as its potential to lower blood sugar levels and reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of Eurya quinquelocularis are typically made by boiling the leaves and stems in water or using them as a decoction, and can be consumed as a tea or used topically as a poultice.
